title: rapanea spathulata
intro: Rapanea spathulata, also known as the yellow wood or yellow wood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is a tree that is native to New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rapanea spathulata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
